description:
You are a freelance web-developer who has just cloned a project that requires a fixed time-zone and locale in every interactive shell.  
Please configure the **non-privileged (user-level) environment** so that a new login shell automatically recognizes the following settings:

• Time zone: `America/New_York`  
• Locale : `en_US.UTF-8`

Once everything is configured, create the single verification log file
```
/home/user/project/time_locale_config.log
```
containing **exactly** the two lines shown below (no additional spaces before/after, and end the file with a single line-feed character):

```
Configured Time Zone: America/New_York
Configured Locale: en_US.UTF-8
```

Details and constraints the automated grader will check:

1. Shell startup file  
   • Append (do not overwrite existing content) the two export statements  
     ```
     export TZ="America/New_York"
     export LC_ALL="en_US.UTF-8"
     ```  
     to the very end of `/home/user/.bashrc`.  
   • These exact key/value pairs must appear literally; quotation marks must be present.

2. Directory layout  
   • Create the directory `/home/user/project` if it does not yet exist.  
   • Place the required log file *directly* inside this directory (not in a sub-directory).

3. Log file format  
   • The first line must be: `Configured Time Zone: America/New_York`  
   • The second line must be: `Configured Locale: en_US.UTF-8`  
   • Do **not** include any blank lines, additional text, or carriage-return characters.  
   • Ensure there is exactly one trailing `\n` (newline) after the second line.

4. Session changes  
   • After modifying `.bashrc`, reload it for the current session so that the variables become immediately available (this guarantees subsequent commands run with the new settings).

Typical workflow (5–10 commands):  
   – Inspect current date/locale, create the project directory, back up `.bashrc`, append the two `export` lines, reload the shell configuration, verify via `echo $TZ` / `locale`, and finally write the verification log file.

The grader validates only:  
   a) the presence and content of `/home/user/project/time_locale_config.log` (exact match as specified above)  
   b) that the two `export` lines are present at the **end** of `/home/user/.bashrc`.

No other output is examined.
truth:
Final expected filesystem state:

1. File: /home/user/.bashrc  
   • Last two non-blank lines are exactly:  
     export TZ="America/New_York"  
     export LC_ALL="en_US.UTF-8"

2. Directory: /home/user/project  
   • Must exist and be accessible by the current user (default perms 755 are fine).

3. File: /home/user/project/time_locale_config.log  
   • Exact byte sequence (including final line-feed):  
     0: 43 6f 6e 66 69 67 75 72 65 64 20 54 69 6d 65 20 5a 6f 6e 65 3a 20 41 6d 65 72 69 63 61 2f 4e 65 77 5f 59 6f 72 6b 0a  
     40: 43 6f 6e 66 69 67 75 72 65 64 20 4c 6f 63 61 6c 65 3a 20 65 6e 5f 55 53 2e 55 54 46 2d 38 0a

   • When decoded as UTF-8-encoded text with Unix line endings:  
     Configured Time Zone: America/New_York  
     Configured Locale: en_US.UTF-8  

   • No additional lines, spaces, or carriage returns.

Automated tests check only these items; they do not rely on environment variables at run-time.
